name: org.machanism.machai:machai-mcp-server
description: Machai MCP Server is a Java 17 Maven artifact that provides the executable Model Context Protocol (MCP) server layer for the Machai AI framework. It exposes dynamically loaded Machai GenAI functions as MCP tools and prompts so MCP clients can discover and invoke AI-powered Java tool providers. The server supports process-based STDIO communication for local integrations and HTTP communication for remote clients backed by Jetty. HTTP deployments include stateless synchronous request handling and streamable, session-aware communication. The package org.machanism.machai.mcp.server contains the command-line entry point, shared server abstractions, transport-specific implementations, and a GenericGenaiAdapter that converts Machai ToolFunction definitions and parameter descriptors into MCP-compatible JSON schemas and tool specifications. During startup, the selected server creates MCP capabilities for tools and prompts, initializes function tool loading, applies available Machai tools through the adapter, converts request arguments to JSON, injects session and project context when available, executes the underlying tool functions, serializes text results, and reports execution failures as MCP error results. This artifact is intended for applications, development environments, IDE assistants, local agent runners, automation systems, and backend services that need a protocol-compatible bridge between MCP clients and Machai tool providers. It can be embedded as a library or run as a ready-to-use command-line server through org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.McpServer with options for help, project directory, server name, server version, port, and HTTP session mode.

usageContext: Expose dynamically loaded Machai GenAI functions as MCP tools and prompts
Run a local MCP server over standard input and output for process-based clients
Run a remote MCP server over HTTP using Jetty
Choose stateless HTTP mode for synchronous request handling
Choose streamable session-aware HTTP mode when an MCP client needs session-oriented communication
Bridge Machai ToolFunction instances and parameter descriptors into MCP-compatible tool schemas
Supply project directory context to loaded tools for project-aware automation
Integrate Java-based AI tool providers with IDE assistants, local agents, automation systems, and backend services

constructors.description: Creates a stateless synchronous HTTP MCP server that listens for HTTP requests on a configured port. Use this constructor when exposing Machai GenAI tools to remote MCP clients without maintaining a streamable session-oriented transport.

examples.description: Creates a stateless HTTP MCP server, configures the current project directory, binds the server to port 8080, registers Machai tools, and starts serving MCP requests for remote clients.

constructors.description: Creates a streamable, session-aware HTTP MCP server. Use this constructor for HTTP deployments where clients need streamable communication and session context in addition to the shared Machai tool registration behavior.

examples.description: Creates a streamable HTTP MCP server, assigns project context, binds to port 8081, registers available GenAI tools, and starts an HTTP endpoint suitable for session-aware MCP clients.

constructors.description: Creates an MCP server that communicates through standard input and standard output. Use this constructor for local process-based integrations where an MCP client launches the Java server as a subprocess.

examples.description: Creates a STDIO MCP server for local integrations, supplies project directory context, registers tools, and starts protocol communication through the process standard streams.

constructors.description: Creates a generic adapter for integrating Machai GenAI tools with different MCP server implementations. The adapter uses the supplied list and builder to convert Machai tool metadata, parameter descriptors, and call handlers into transport-specific MCP tool specifications.

examples.description: Shows the creation pattern for a GenericGenaiAdapter. In a real transport implementation, replace the null builder with a concrete ToolSpecificationBuilder that creates MCP specification objects for the chosen exchange type.

features.name: main
features.description: Command-line entry point for starting the MCP server. It parses options for help, project directory, server name, server version, HTTP port, and session mode. Without a port it starts STDIO mode; with a port it starts HTTP mode; with both port and session mode it uses streamable HTTP behavior.

examples.description: Runs the ready-to-use server in STDIO mode for a local MCP client because no port is supplied. The projectDir option gives loaded tools a project context.
Runs the ready-to-use server over HTTP on port 8080 and enables streamable session-aware HTTP mode. Omit --session to use stateless HTTP mode.

features.name: tools
features.description: Registers available Machai GenAI tools and prompt capabilities with the selected MCP server. During this step, tool parameter metadata is converted into MCP-compatible JSON schema properties so clients can discover and invoke tools correctly.

examples.description: Registers tools before the server is started. This is required when embedding the server so loaded Machai ToolFunction instances become visible as MCP tools.

features.name: start
features.description: Starts the configured MCP server transport. Concrete implementations start either STDIO communication, stateless HTTP handling, or streamable HTTP handling after tools and prompts have been configured.

examples.description: Starts a configured stateless HTTP server on port 8080 after project context and tool registration are complete.

features.name: setProjectDir
features.description: Sets the project directory used by the MCP server and loaded Machai tools. This enables project-aware tool execution, configuration lookup, and filesystem-oriented automation from the selected project root.

examples.description: Configures the server to execute loaded tools against a specific project directory before registering tools and starting the transport.

features.name: setPort
features.description: Configures the TCP port used by HTTP MCP server implementations. This applies to stateless HTTP and streamable HTTP deployments backed by Jetty servlet hosting logic.

examples.description: Sets a custom HTTP port before startup so an MCP client can connect to the server at the expected network endpoint.

features.name: addTool
features.signature: void org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.GenericGenaiAdapter.addTool(java.lang.String name, java.lang.String description, org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction toolFunction, org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ParamDescriptor... paramDescriptors)
features.description: Adds a Machai tool to the generic adapter and creates an MCP-visible tool specification. The adapter builds JSON schema properties from parameter descriptors, routes MCP tool calls to the supplied ToolFunction, passes request arguments and session context when available, and returns MCP-compliant results.

examples.description: Demonstrates the adapter registration workflow. A production integration supplies a real ToolSpecificationBuilder, ToolFunction, and parameter descriptors so the tool becomes discoverable and callable through MCP.

customizations.name: AbstractMcpServer.ToolSpecificationBuilder
customizations.description: Interface for building tool and tool specification objects for MCP servers. It is the main transport abstraction point that allows the same logical Machai tool model to be converted into transport-specific MCP specification objects for STDIO, stateless HTTP, streamable HTTP, or future server implementations.

examples.description: Use this contract when implementing a custom transport or exchange model that needs to build its own MCP tool specification objects from generic Machai tool metadata.

customizations.name: AbstractMcpServer
customizations.description: Abstract base class for MCP server implementations. It defines shared server metadata, project directory handling, tool registration behavior, startup behavior, and the tool specification builder contract used by transport-specific implementations.

examples.description: Extend this base class when creating a new MCP server implementation that should reuse Machai server metadata, project context handling, and tool registration architecture.

customizations.name: AbstractHttpMcpServer
customizations.description: Abstract base class for HTTP-based MCP server implementations. It supplies the Jetty server, connector, servlet context, and HTTP transport servlet hosting logic used by remote MCP deployments.

examples.description: Extend this class when creating an HTTP transport variant that should reuse the common Jetty and servlet infrastructure while supplying transport-specific tool specification behavior.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractMcpServer.ToolSpecificationBuilder
studs.description: Interface for building tool and tool specification objects for MCP servers. It is intended for implementation by transport-specific builders that convert Machai tool definitions into concrete MCP specification instances.

examples.description: Represents the extension contract used by GenericGenaiAdapter and concrete server implementations to create transport-specific MCP tool specifications.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractMcpServer
studs.description: Abstract base class for MCP server implementations. It centralizes common metadata, project-directory handling, tool registration, and startup behavior for STDIO and HTTP server modes.

examples.description: Use this abstract class as the superclass for a new MCP server transport that should participate in the same Machai tool registration and lifecycle model.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractHttpMcpServer
studs.description: Abstract base class for HTTP-based MCP server implementations. It provides shared Jetty and servlet hosting behavior for stateless and streamable HTTP MCP servers.

examples.description: Use this abstract class as the foundation for an HTTP MCP transport that needs the common Jetty connector and servlet context setup.

examples.title: Install and run the ready-to-use MCP server in STDIO mode
examples.scenario: Use the artifact as a local subprocess launched by an MCP client, IDE assistant, or desktop AI tool.
steps: Install the component by adding the Maven dependency org.machanism.machai:machai-mcp-server:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT to a Java 17 application or obtain the built JAR produced by the package profile.
Ensure the runtime classpath includes machai-mcp-server and its dependencies, including the MCP SDK, genai-client, commons-cli, commons-text, SLF4J, Logback, and Jetty dependencies when needed.
Configure the project directory with --projectDir so loaded tools have the correct project context.
Run org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.McpServer without --port to use STDIO mode.
Configure the MCP client to launch this Java command and communicate through standard input and output.
examples.description: This example shows installation through Maven dependency coordinates and invocation of the ready-to-run server in STDIO mode. It is the default mode when no port is supplied and is appropriate for process-based MCP clients.

examples.title: Install, configure, and run the HTTP MCP server
examples.scenario: Expose Machai tools to remote MCP clients over HTTP using stateless or streamable session-aware transport.
steps: Add the Maven dependency org.machanism.machai:machai-mcp-server:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT or build the executable JAR with the Maven assembly package profile.
Choose a port for the HTTP endpoint, such as 8080.
Set --projectDir to the project whose context should be passed to loaded tools.
Run McpServer with --port for stateless HTTP mode.
Add --session when the MCP client requires streamable session-aware HTTP communication.
examples.snippet:
java -cp machai-mcp-server-1.2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.McpServer --projectDir . --name "Machai MCP Server" --version "1.2.0-SNAPSHOT" --port 8080

java -cp machai-mcp-server-1.2.0-SNAPSHOT.jar org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.McpServer --projectDir . --name "Machai MCP Server" --version "1.2.0-SNAPSHOT" --port 8080 --session
examples.description: The first command starts the HTTP server in stateless mode. The second command enables streamable session-aware HTTP mode. Both commands expose dynamically loaded Machai tools and prompts to MCP clients over a Jetty-backed HTTP server.

examples.title: Embed a stateless HTTP MCP server in Java code
examples.scenario: Use the library API directly inside a Java application that hosts an MCP endpoint.
steps: Add the Maven dependency to the host application.
Create org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.HttpStatelessMcpServer with a server name and version.
Configure project directory and HTTP port.
Call tools() to load and expose Machai GenAI tools.
Call start() to begin serving MCP requests.
examples.description: This how-to-use scenario embeds the server rather than launching the command-line entry point. It is useful for backend services or automation runtimes that manage the MCP server lifecycle programmatically.

examples.title: Register custom Machai tools through GenericGenaiAdapter
examples.scenario: Bridge a Machai ToolFunction into an MCP-visible tool specification for a custom server composition.
steps: Create a list to hold generated transport-specific MCP tool specifications.
Provide an org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.AbstractMcpServer.ToolSpecificationBuilder implementation for the chosen exchange type.
Construct org.machanism.machai.mcp.server.GenericGenaiAdapter with the list and builder.
Call addTool with the tool name, description, ToolFunction, and ParamDescriptor values.
Attach the generated specifications to the MCP server transport.
examples.description: This example shows the adapter pattern described by the Javadocs. In real usage, the builder and ToolFunction are concrete implementations; the adapter converts parameter descriptors into JSON schema properties and routes MCP calls to the tool function.
